security.label: Unbreakable security
security.title: You alone control access
security.intro: Every MYKEYNEST extension installation generates a unique cryptographic fingerprint (hash). Access is strictly tied to your instance — impossible to impersonate, even with your credentials.

points.p1_text: Access is tied to your specific instance. An attacker on another device is blocked even with your credentials.
points.p2_text: If the extension is modified or tampered with, the hash changes instantly and access is revoked.
points.p3_text: Only integrity proofs are recorded server-side — never sensitive data itself.
points.p4_text: Every access attempt is logged with a cryptographic timestamp for your security audits.

faq.label: Frequently asked questions
faq.title: Your questions about MYKEYNEST
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before getting started.

items.a1: It is a tool that stores and encrypts all your credentials in a secure digital vault. MYKEYNEST uses AES-256 encryption, the same standard used by banks.
items.a2: Yes. Your data is encrypted locally on your device before being synchronized. We never have access to your passwords in plain text (zero-knowledge architecture).
items.q3: Is the free plan really free?
items.a3: Yes, no credit card required. You can use MYKEYNEST for free with basic features. The Pro plan unlocks advanced features.
items.q4: Can I use MYKEYNEST on multiple devices?
items.a4: Yes, your passwords are automatically synchronized across all your devices: computer, smartphone, and tablet.
items.q5: What is a hashed installation and how does it protect me?
items.a5: Every MYKEYNEST extension installation generates a unique cryptographic fingerprint tied to your instance. If an attacker tries to reuse your credentials on another device, the hash won't match and access is denied. If the extension is modified or corrupted, the integrity check fails and the vault locks automatically.
items.q6: What happens if I forget my master password?
items.a6: We cannot recover it for you (zero-knowledge). That's why it's essential to keep it safe. You can set up emergency recovery.
items.a7: Yes. MYKEYNEST is a 100% European solution: your data is hosted in Europe and never transferred to third-party servers outside the EU. The zero-knowledge architecture ensures we never have access to your passwords in plain text. GDPR compliance is built-in, not an afterthought.
items.a8: 2FA adds an extra layer of security: in addition to your master password, a temporary code is required at each login. Available via an authenticator app (TOTP) or email.

3. Click Load Temporary Add-on

seo.title: How to create a truly secure password?
seo.intro: The security of your online accounts starts with strong passwords. Our free password generator uses advanced cryptographic algorithms to create unique and unguessable combinations.
seo.good_password_text: A secure password should combine several essential elements. It should be long enough (at least 12 characters), mix different character types (uppercase, lowercase, numbers and symbols), and above all be unique for each account.
seo.why_generator_text: Human-created passwords are often predictable and vulnerable. Our tool generates completely random passwords that provide optimal protection against brute-force attacks and hacking attempts. Each generated password is unique and cryptographically secure.
tips: Use a different password for each important account
Prefer passwords of at least 16 characters
Use a secure password manager like MYKEYNEST
Never share your passwords via email or SMS
seo.tech_text: Our generator uses your browser’s native Web Crypto API to produce truly random numbers. Unlike classic pseudo-random generators, this method guarantees maximum entropy and professional-grade cryptographic security.

cta.title: Ready to secure your accounts?
cta.description: Join thousands of users who protect their data with MYKEYNEST

popular.title: Frequently asked questions
popular.sub: The most viewed resources by our community.

write.title: Write to us
write.desc: A specific question? Our team replies in under 2 hours on weekdays.

items.q1: Are my passwords really secure with MYKEYNEST?
items.a1: Yes. Your data is encrypted locally with AES-256 before being sent to our servers. We use a zero-knowledge architecture: even our teams cannot read your passwords. Only your master password can decrypt them.
items.q2: Can I use MYKEYNEST on multiple devices?
items.a2: Absolutely. Multi-device sync is included in all plans. Your credentials are available on computer, smartphone, and tablet — always end-to-end encrypted.
items.q3: Does the password generator store my passwords?
items.a3: No. The generator runs entirely client-side using the browser's crypto.getRandomValues API. No data is sent to our servers. The password disappears as soon as you close the tab.
items.q4: How do I upgrade to Pro?
items.a4: From your personal space, go to Settings › Subscription and click “Upgrade to Pro”. Payment is securely handled by Stripe. Your account is upgraded instantly with no downtime.
items.a5: Yes. The source code is publicly available on GitHub, allowing the community to audit security and contribute. Transparency is one of our core principles.

meta.title: Enterprise Password Manager for SMBs | MYKEYNEST
meta.description: Secure your SMB access with an enterprise password manager built for secure sharing, access audits, compliance, and phishing risk reduction.
meta.keywords: enterprise password manager, business password manager, SMB password manager, secure passwords for SMBs, password sharing for teams, access audit business

hero.subtitle: Centralize business passwords, share access without friction, reduce phishing risk, and keep clear visibility on who can access what.

why.title: Why an SMB needs an enterprise password manager
why.subtitle: As soon as a team shares tools, cloud accounts, or client access, passwords become an IT security issue, not just a convenience issue.

card1.title: Scattered passwords create blind spots
card1.text: Spreadsheets, emails, chat tools, and unmanaged browsers lead to untracked access, password reuse, and poor offboarding when employees leave.

card2.title: Access sharing must stay controlled
card2.text: SMBs need to hand over access quickly without exposing accounts. Secure sharing avoids sensitive credentials being sent through unsafe channels.

card3.title: SMB cybersecurity often starts with access control
card3.text: A compromised account often opens the door to phishing, fraud, or ransomware. An enterprise password manager helps secure this first critical layer.

why.band_text: If you are looking for a practical way to secure SMB passwords without deploying a heavy stack, this page answers that exact need.

features.title: What a good business password manager should actually cover
features.subtitle: The goal is not just to store passwords, but to create a simple, secure, and usable access framework for the whole team.

card1.title: Credential protection
card1.item2: Detection of weak or reused passwords

card2.title: Secure sharing between teammates
card2.item3: Simpler revocation and rotation of access

card3.title: Audit, compliance, and control
card3.item1: Traceability of usage and shared access
card3.item2: A stronger base for GDPR and internal procedures
card3.item3: Better password hygiene across the SMB

steps.title: How to deploy an enterprise password manager without friction
steps.subtitle: The goal is not to change every habit overnight, but to build a secure baseline quickly.

step1.title: Map critical access points
step1.text: List the tools, shared accounts, cloud consoles, mailboxes, and sensitive access already used in the business.

step2.title: Centralize risky credentials first
step2.text: Start with exposed accounts: business tools, finance, hosting, marketing, support, and client environments.

step3.title: Define sharing and rotation rules
step3.text: Decide who can view, share, or rotate access in order to reduce gray areas and single-person dependencies.

step4.title: Train the team on the right habits
step4.text: Pair the password manager with practical rules: unique passwords, phishing awareness, MFA, and timely access removal.

schema.name: SMB Cybersecurity Audit & Training
schema.description: Cybersecurity audit and employee training service for SMBs. Remote or on-site audit, vulnerability report and phishing awareness.

hero.subtitle: 91% of cyberattacks start with human error. We audit your vulnerabilities and train your teams so it doesn't happen to you.

proof.stat1: of cyberattacks start with human error (source: IBM)
proof.stat3: average cost of a cyberattack for an SMB (source: ANSSI)

services.title: Two complementary services
services.subtitle: The audit identifies your vulnerabilities, training protects your teams. Together, they form your cybersecurity shield.

audit.title: Security audit
audit.desc: Complete analysis of your infrastructure to identify vulnerabilities before they are exploited.

training.title: Employee training
training.desc: Your teams are your first line of defense. We train them to recognize and avoid the most common threats.

process.title: Our 4-step method
process.subtitle: From first contact to final report, here's how we work with your SMB.

step1.title: Contact & initial assessment
step1.desc: A 30-minute call to understand your context, challenges and define the scope of the engagement.

step2.title: Security audit
step2.desc: Analysis of your systems, password policies, access rights and infrastructure. Remote or on-site as needed.

step3.title: Team training
step3.desc: Awareness session tailored to your sector: phishing, social engineering, day-to-day best practices.

step4.title: Report & remediation plan
step4.desc: Delivery of a clear report with identified vulnerabilities, their criticality level and priority actions to take.

q1.question: How long does an audit take?
q1.answer: A remote audit typically takes 2 to 3 business days. An on-site audit spans half a day to a full day depending on the size of your organisation. The report is delivered within 5 days of the engagement.

q2.question: Do I need MYKEYNEST to benefit from the audit?
q2.answer: No. The audit and training are offered independently of our software. Of course, if the audit reveals password management issues, we can propose MYKEYNEST as a suitable solution.

q3.question: Is the training suitable for non-technical employees?
q3.answer: Absolutely. Our training is designed for non-technical profiles: sales, HR, accounting… We use real-world examples from your sector to maximise understanding and retention.

q4.question: What is the cost of an audit?
q4.answer: The price varies depending on the scope and size of your company. Contact us for a free personalised quote. As a MYKEYNEST Pro customer, you benefit from a built-in software security audit at no extra cost.

cta.title: Ready to audit your security?
cta.subtitle: Contact us for a free 30-minute initial assessment.
